name: Doppler Effect: Wave Frequency Shift
description:
The Doppler effect is the change in frequency of a wave in relation to an observer who is moving relative to the wave source. This simulation visualizes how sound waves compress or expand based on the relative velocities of the source and the observer.

### The Doppler Formula
The observed frequency $f'$ is calculated as:

$$f' = f \left( \frac{v - v_o}{v - v_s} \right)$$

Where:
- $f$ is the emitted frequency
- $v$ is the speed of sound in the medium
- $v_o$ is the velocity of the observer (positive if moving away from the source)
- $v_s$ is the velocity of the source (positive if moving towards the observer)
